feedback.text: Hello together, please also consider the topic in the Digital Fairness Act: Stop Killing Games. More and more games bought digitally disappear simply later, e.g. when servers are switched off or companies delete them from the library (see Overwatch 1, Assassins Creed Liberation, Nintendo eShop, etc.). This is unfair to consumers. Anyone who buys a game should also be able to play it permanently, at least offline or as an archive version. The DFA is already committed to transparency and fair digital contracts. It would be superfluous to include this point as well. Thank you very much. A concerned EU citizen
